description_long: This is a stylized pitchfork with three prominent, evenly spaced tines at the top, each tapering to a sharp point. The tines and shaft are rendered in a metallic gold color, giving the object a regal and decorative appearance. At the base of the tines, there is a blue gem-like ornament set into the fork's head, adding a touch of elegance and fantasy. The shaft is long and slender, with a black grip section in the middle for comfortable handling. The bottom of the shaft ends in a pointed tip, mirroring the sharpness of the tines above. The overall design suggests a ceremonial or decorative use, rather than a purely utilitarian tool.
description: A gold, three-tined pitchfork with a blue gem at the head and a black grip on the shaft, featuring a pointed tip at the base.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all three gold tines, the blue gem at the head, and the black grip on the shaft.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the tines and shaft, with the blue gem visible in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the three tines, blue gem, and black grip, but with less detail on the gem.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the tines and shaft, with the blue gem in profile.
